name: Harp of the Dagda
description: The Harp of the Dagda, also known as Uaithne, was a magical harp belonging to the Dagda, a prominent and powerful god in Irish mythology. This harp was not merely a musical instrument; it possessed potent abilities that influenced emotions and controlled the seasons. Forged with exceptional craftsmanship, the harp's music could evoke joy, sorrow, or even induce sleep. It was said to play itself at the Dagda's command, summoning the appropriate tune for any occasion. A particularly significant tale recounts how the harp was stolen by the Fomorians, the Dagda's enemies. The Dagda, along with Lugh and Oghma, tracked the harp to their stronghold. Upon finding it, the Dagda commanded the harp to play itself, which it did, lulling the Fomorians into a deep slumber. This allowed the Tuatha Dé Danann to retrieve the artifact, showcasing its crucial role in their battles and dominance. The harp symbolizes the Dagda's mastery over music, magic, and the natural world, representing a vital instrument in maintaining balance and order within the mythical landscape of ancient Ireland. Its disappearance from the historical record adds to its mystique, leaving its current whereabouts unknown and fueling speculation about its continued existence in some hidden realm.
subtitle: A magical instrument of immense power
material: Unknown, possibly magical wood and precious metals
origin: Created by skilled artisans for the Dagda

keyMyths.title: Theft by the Fomorians
keyMyths.description: The Fomorians stole the Harp of the Dagda, but the Dagda, Lugh, and Oghma tracked it down and retrieved it using the harp's magical music to lull the Fomorians to sleep.

keyMyths.title: Control of the Seasons
keyMyths.description: The Harp of the Dagda was said to have the power to control the seasons, with its music able to bring about summer, winter, or any other time of year.
keyMyths.source: D'Arbois de Jubainville, Henri. The Irish Mythological Cycle.

sources.author: Cross, Tom Peete
sources.description: A comprehensive index of motifs found in early Irish literature, including references to the Harp of the Dagda.
sources.text: A comprehensive index of motifs found in early Irish literature, including references to the Harp of the Dagda.

sources.author: D'Arbois de Jubainville, Henri
sources.description: A detailed analysis of the major cycles of Irish mythology, including the role of the Dagda and his harp.
